data.content:
The build confirms the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file successfully, but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. MSBuild's implicit globbing only captures files that exist at evaluation time (before targets run), so build-generated files are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` and `FileWrites` within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The problem is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing for `Compile` items happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so files created during the build aren't automatically picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also to `FileWrites` for clean support.

This is the classic "including generated files" pattern. The target needs to add:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** MSBuild glob-based `<Compile>` items expand at *evaluation time* — before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the `.cs` file during *execution time*, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `Compile` (so Roslyn compiles it) and `FileWrites` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
